name: Chocolate and Pear Tartlets
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift flour.
2. Cut butter into 6 tablespoons and pound between two sheets of wax paper until soft.
3. Make a well in the center of the flour mixture. Add sugar, salt, and vanilla to the well.
4. Pour butter into the well and mix with your fingertips until thoroughly combined.
5. Add egg yolks to the well and incorporate them into the ingredients.
6. Fold in the remaining flour using a metal spatula until coarse crumbs form.
7. Form the dough into a ball, dust lightly with flour on a work surface, and press into a disk. Cover and chill for 30 minutes.
8. Brush tartlet molds with melted butter. Place four molds together so their edges nearly touch.
9. Divide dough in half and roll out to about 1/8". Roll loosely around the rolling pin and drape over the molds to cover them. Use a piece of dough from the edge, form into a ball, dust with flour, and press into the flutes of each mold.
10. Roll the rolling pin over the tops of the molds to cut away excess dough. Bake at 400°F (200°C).
11. Chop chocolate finely and sprinkle into each shell.
12. Whisk egg, cream, and kirsch together until well mixed. Spoon about 2-3 tablespoons over the chocolate.
13. Peel pears, halve, and remove cores. Slice thinly and arrange in a flower-petal design on the custard, overlapping slightly. Press down lightly into the custard and sprinkle evenly with sugar.
14. Place on a baking sheet near the bottom of the oven and bake for 10 minutes. Reduce temperature to 350°F (175°C) and continue baking until pastry is golden and custard is set, about 15-20 more minutes.
Coulis:
1. Puree raspberries in a processor.
2. Add kirsch (if using) and mix well.
